case_title: CACV OF COLORADO, LLC VS. JAIME B LIEBERMAN et al

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF NON SERVICE: NTC OF HEARING ON PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD; MEMO OF P&A; PROPOSED ORDER; JUDGMENT FILED BY PETITIONER CACV OF COLORADO, LLC

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER CACV OF COLORADO, LLC'S PETITION TO CONFRIM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-14-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER CACV OF COLORADO, LLC RECOVER FROM RESPONDENT LIEBERMAN, JAIME B $19,253.21 PRINCIPAL, $525.00 ATTORNEY FEES, $5,934.02 INTEREST, $535.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $26,247.23, (JUDGMENT RE CONFIRMED ARBITRATION AWARD) SEE SCANNED DOC

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confrim Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MARK GOOSSENS, ESQ., (415)984-0430, FOR PETITIONER/MOVING PARTY, CACV OF COLORADO, LLC
NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY THE RESPONDENT/OPPOSING PARTY, JAIME B. LIEBERMAN

THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER CA CV OF COLORADO, LLC'S Petition To Confrim Arbitration Award IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar May-21-2008 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, And Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-06-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Philip T. Kilduff, 673-0368, specially appearing for petitioner and moving party

Petitioner's request for a continuance was denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER CACV OF COLORADO, LLC'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is denied for failure to provide Proof of Service. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
